subject: Disable RTC error messages.
author: Sorgelig

upstream.vanilla_quote: rtc-m41t80.c line 215: dev_dbg(&client->dev, "Unable to read date\n"); i2c-designware-master.c line 857: dev_err(dev->dev, "controller timed out\n");

provenance_doc_ref: Class C (line 348) for m41t80 half + Class F-5 (line 399) for i2c-designware half

impact.effect_if_absent: Spurious dev_err messages logged to kernel ring buffer on boot when optional RTC add-on board not fitted; no functional impact on boards with RTC hardware present

notes: CRITICAL SPLIT COMMIT — This single commit touches two files with different upstream status and is split across two provenance doc rows: (1) m41t80 half: DROPPED-UPSTREAM via c7622a4e44d9 (Alexandre Belloni, 2025-05-26, 'rtc: m41t80: reduce verbosity') — verified at 6.18.38 drivers/rtc/rtc-m41t80.c:215 already contains dev_dbg; (2) i2c-designware half: CARRIED via patch 0030-i2c-designware-quiet-timeout.patch — verified at 6.18.38 drivers/i2c/busses/i2c-designware-master.c:857 still contains dev_err, confirming need for carry. Patch 0030 header explicitly documents this split and notes forward-port work (Michael C. Ferguson, 2026-07-12): i2c_dw_xfer() was refactored upstream into i2c_dw_wait_transfer() helper; the same dev_err→dev_dbg change applied at new call site. Effect: cosmetic silencing of boot spam when no RTC add-on fitted; no functional loss, hardware support intact. No userspace coupling. Patch applies cleanly. ESCALATE FOR AUDITOR REVIEW: This represents a split-commit handling pattern (one logical commit, two rows in provenance table, mixed disposition) that may require policy clarification.
